title: Mastery Exam: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders
summary: Mastery Exam: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders retrieves prior skills in an original independent decorative-layer and state release scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css list styling, css link states, css backgrounds borders in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent decorative-layer and state release artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey artifact and connect styles.css. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Build the survey design system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: survey verification checklist redesign · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent decorative-layer and state release.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent decorative-layer and state release.

content.title: independent decorative-layer and state release evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent decorative-layer and state release case 1: removing bullets always removes list semantics from every browser and screen reader.
Independent decorative-layer and state release case 1: preserve list markup, make grouping visually clear, and verify semantics plus marker alignment after styling.
Independent decorative-layer and state release case 1: replace every list item with a paragraph and a manually typed bullet character.
